siteSubtitle: José Rizal in the Philippines and in Germany
homeTitle: The Hero, Artist, and Ethnographer: José Rizal Digital Exhibition
description: This digital exhibition reflects on Rizal's life, offering a glimpse into his many facets beyond the renowned hero. A gifted artist, an ethnographer championing Filipino culture, and an exceptional scholar.

homeQuote.text: Ang paaralan ang aklat kung saan nakasulat ang kinabukasan ng bayan.

homeTeasers.heading: Rizal in Germany
homeTeasers.text: José Rizal traveled to Germany to pursue his specialization in ophthalmology. Little did he know that his stay, particularly in Berlin, would soon open his eyes to a broader perspective and worldview. From finishing Noli Me Tángere to engaging with leading scientists, Rizal came to understand his identity more clearly.

image.alt: Bronze statue of José Rizal, hand to chin and holding a book, on a plaza before forested German hills

homeTeasers.heading: About the Project
homeTeasers.text: This digital exhibition reflects on Rizal's life, offering a glimpse into his many facets beyond the renowned hero: a gifted artist, an ethnographer championing Filipino culture, and an exceptional scholar.

footer.aboutText: The National Museum of the Philippines is an educational, scientific, and cultural institution that acquires, documents, preserves, exhibits, and fosters scholarly studies and public appreciation of works of art, specimens, and cultural and historical artifacts representative of the unique cultural heritage of the Filipino people and natural history of the Philippines.
